title: Honda CR-V oil change (first 5 min)
summary: The operator is performing an oil change on a Honda CRV (2000s model). They identify several previous maintenance issues including incorrect screws, missing clips, a misrouted hose, and an aftermarket compressor that was likely replaced unnecessarily. The draining oil is black and chunky, indicating poor condition. The operator uses various tools including a gear wrench ratchet and an OTC drain plug remover. They also highlight features of Toolcraft ratchet organizers. A minor grease leak from seals is noted but dismissed as normal manufacturer's grease.

events.label: Vehicle Identification
events.detail: Honda CRV (year ~2000s) identified on a lift in a workshop.

events.label: Part Anomaly
events.detail: Incorrect screw identified on the underside of the vehicle.

events.label: Part Anomaly
events.detail: Missing screw identified on the underside of the vehicle.

events.label: Part Anomaly
events.detail: Missing clips (fasteners) found on the vehicle's underside protection panel.

events.label: Work Progress Note
events.detail: Technician comments on a 'poor start' to the oil change due to missing parts.

events.label: Component Observation
events.detail: Aftermarket compressor observed. Technician suspects original compressor replaced unnecessarily.

events.label: Part Anomaly
events.detail: Misrouted hose observed near the compressor. This hose was also replaced, which the technician doubts was necessary.

events.label: Structural Anomaly
events.detail: Excessive flex and a large gap observed near the clutch/compressor area, indicating potential improper installation.

events.label: Component Observation
events.detail: New aftermarket drain plug found on the oil pan.

events.label: Tool Selection
events.detail: Technician retrieves a gear wrench flex-head ratchet from the toolbox.

events.label: Tool Demonstration
events.detail: Toolcraft ratchet organizers are showcased, highlighting magnetic and interlocking features for organization.

events.label: Maintenance Procedure
events.detail: Technician begins removing the oil drain plug.

events.label: Fluid Condition
events.detail: Engine oil draining, described as 'chromium swirl chunky milk,' indicating very poor oil quality.

events.label: Tool Use
events.detail: OTC Drain Plug Pro (magnetic tool) used to remove the oil filter without making a mess, though some oil does spill.

events.label: Part Anomaly
events.detail: Missing a 10mm bolt from a bracket, adding to the list of previous maintenance deficiencies.

events.label: Component Condition
events.detail: Grease observed seeping from seals, identified as normal manufacturer's grease during break-in, not an oil leak.
